title: Expose ping via Cloudflare quick tunnel + verify reachable over public HTTPS

brief.why: Quick tunnel needs no auth -> proves public reachability today without interactive login.
brief.what: A public https://*.trycloudflare.com URL forwarding to local ping
brief.done: MCP client against the public URL returns a deterministic pong
brief.how: cloudflared tunnel --url http://127.0.0.1:8791 -> grab the trycloudflare.com URL. Point the streamable-HTTP MCP client at <url>/mcp and confirm pong over the PUBLIC URL.

title: Wire the Claude plugin wrapper + marketplace.json

brief.why: Claude is the outlier — it needs a plugin wrapper with semver, not just .agents/ projection. This proves the Claude install path and sets up the anti-greyed-button semver discipline.
brief.done: plugin.json validates; marketplace.json uses relative source; flattened skill is a real file (not symlink).
brief.how: plugins/couvert/.claude-plugin/plugin.json (name, version 0.1.0, description, mcpServers.ping using ${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT}). plugins/couvert/skills/couvert/SKILL.md as FLATTENED real file (from scripts/flatten.py, not symlink). plugins/couvert/.mcp.json. .claude-plugin/marketplace.json with relative source ./plugins/couvert (org-sync-safe).

title: Make it permanent: named tunnel (ping.planetmodha.com) + systemd units

brief.why: Quick tunnels are ephemeral; a real connector needs a stable URL. Phase B.
brief.what: Stable ping.planetmodha.com served by systemd-managed tunnel + ping
brief.done: ping.planetmodha.com/mcp serves pong across restarts; connector stable
brief.how: Needs Sameer's interactive cloudflared tunnel login (browser, planetmodha.com). Then create ping tunnel; route dns; config.yml hostname->127.0.0.1:8791; systemd --user units for ping + tunnel; re-point connector.

title: Register the URL as a claude.ai custom connector + verify ping callable in chat

brief.why: The Stage-1 benchmark: ping answering from web chat, proving stdio->HTTP is the only gate.
brief.what: ping registered as a claude.ai connector and invoked in chat
brief.done: A claude.ai chat calls ping and shows the pong
brief.how: Add tunnel URL (+/mcp) as a custom/remote MCP connector in claude.ai settings (Sameer drives or via passe). Call ping in a claude.ai chat.

title: Stage 3 — Adding the next tool is one command, from a reusable template

brief.why: The skeleton only pays off if the next tool is cheap to add and impossible to drift. Making it a GitHub template + scaffold command is what turns a one-off proof into reusable infrastructure.
brief.what: A GitHub template repo + a scaffold command that adds a new canonical skill/MCP and wires it into all generated views in one step.
brief.done: From the template, one scaffold command adds a new tool that appears in all three agents after 'make sync'; 'make release' bumps semver and tags it.
brief.how: Convert the repo into a GitHub template; add a 'new-tool' scaffold (via trousse:scaffold) so adding a tool is one command and updating is 'make sync && make release' (CI bumps semver + tags). This is where the recursive 'skill that maintains the skeleton' belongs — deferred from Stage 0 so the smoke-test tool stays orthogonal to its plumbing.

title: Install cloudflared on hezza (user-local binary, no sudo)

brief.why: Tunnel needs the cloudflared client; hezza has none. Install to ~/.local/bin to avoid sudo.
brief.how: curl the linux-arm64 cloudflared binary from GitHub releases to ~/.local/bin/cloudflared, chmod +x, verify --version.

title: Run the two org-sync experiments: symlink survival + submodule recursion

brief.why: These pick subtree vs CI-flatten for Stage 2. The plan slates them in Stage 0 but they require a REAL org-marketplace sync (private repo + Cowork GitHub App, Team/Enterprise) — they cannot run purely locally on hezza.
brief.what: Empirical answers to symlink-survival and submodule-recursion, recorded in understanding.md / the Stage-2 brief.
brief.done: Both experiments run against a real org sync with recorded outcomes; subtree-vs-flatten decision confirmed.
brief.how: BLOCKED on org access. Needs a real org-marketplace sync (private repo + Cowork GitHub App, Team/Enterprise). Cannot run locally on hezza; overlaps Stage 2. Against a private throwaway marketplace repo connected as an org marketplace: (a) install a plugin whose skill is a SYMLINK, trigger a sync, check populated vs empty; (b) install a plugin whose skill folder is a git SUBMODULE, trigger a sync, check recursion. Until org access is wired, the safe default stands: flatten to real files.

waiting_for: Org-marketplace access (private repo + Cowork GitHub App, Team/Enterprise) not wired on hezza; overlaps Stage 2.

title: Author canonical sources: couvert skill, ping MCP, standing instructions

brief.why: These are the ONLY human-edited files (the iron rule). Everything else is generated from them. Getting the canonical shape right is the foundation of the anti-drift design.
brief.done: ping server importable and runs over stdio (returns deterministic pong); couvert SKILL.md has valid frontmatter; AGENTS.md/CLAUDE.md present.
brief.how: skills/couvert/SKILL.md: YAML frontmatter (name + description, description-triggered open/close ritual) + body from build-plan.md PART 4. mcp/ping/: uv project — pyproject.toml (name ping-server, [project.scripts] ping-server = 'ping_server.server:main', deps mcp>=1.2), src/ping_server/server.py (FastMCP, single deterministic ping() tool). AGENTS.md: canonical standing instructions (Codex+Antigravity native). CLAUDE.md: thin — point at @AGENTS.md + Claude-only lines. Verify 'uvx --from ./mcp/ping ping-server' resolves.

title: Stage 2 — A version bump propagates to every org member, drift made impossible

brief.why: This is the structural fix for the bug that started the project: a semver bump + merge must reach all org members on their next session, with no chance of a vendored copy lagging. It turns the Mise greyed-out-Update failure into something that cannot happen.
brief.what: A private org-marketplace repo whose plugins are flattened real files, auto-syncing on merge, with semver enforced.
brief.done: A semver bump merged to the private repo reaches all org members on next session (the Mise bug demonstrably impossible). The two Stage-0 org-sync experiments (symlink survival, submodule recursion) are answered empirically against this real sync.
brief.how: Private repo + marketplace.json with ./plugins/x relative sources + 'Sync automatically' on (re-syncs on PR merge / push via the Cowork GitHub App). Use git subtree or a CI-flatten step to put real plugin content in the tree — NOT submodule, unless the Stage-0 recursion experiment proves server-side recursion. Bump semver on every change; cut <plugin>--vX.Y.Z tags.

title: Run ping as an HTTP service on hezza (Phase A bg, Phase B systemd)

brief.why: The tunnel forwards to a local ping HTTP server. Same canonical binary, PING_TRANSPORT=http.
brief.what: A ping HTTP server listening on 127.0.0.1:8791
brief.done: curl/MCP client against 127.0.0.1:8791/mcp gets a pong
brief.how: Phase A: background uv run --directory ./mcp/ping ping-server with PING_TRANSPORT=http FASTMCP_PORT=8791. Phase B: systemd --user unit (like dolt-bon.service). Bind 127.0.0.1:8791.

title: Stage 1 — The ping tool answers from claude.ai web chat over HTTPS

brief.why: stdio reaches CLI and desktop but NOT the claude.ai web surface. Proving the stdio→HTTP split is the only gate to chat closes the reachability question for every future tool that wants to be callable from the browser.
brief.what: A publicly reachable HTTPS endpoint serving the ping MCP, registered as a claude.ai custom connector.
brief.done: ping is callable inside a claude.ai web chat and returns its deterministic pong.
brief.how: Deploy the SAME canonical ping (one source, two transports) to a public HTTPS host, then add as a claude.ai custom connector. ping's main() already honours $PORT/$HOST and PING_TRANSPORT=http (proven by scripts/smoke_ping_http.py loopback). FIRST MOVE = pick the host/runtime, because it forks the iron rule: (a) deploy the Python ping to a Python-capable host (Cloudflare Container / Fly / Render) -> ONE source, faithful; (b) TS Cloudflare Worker, Carnet-style (agents SDK McpAgent) -> real URL + free tier + fast, BUT ping gets a TS twin = a SECOND source that can drift, only acceptable if we consciously declare ping the one tool allowed per-runtime twins and write that down. Sameer leaning toward a teeny CF Worker like Carnet. Loopback (Stage 0) already proves the transport; Stage 1 is purely reachability + connector registration.

title: Stage 0 — One edit projects a tool into Claude, Codex & Antigravity locally

brief.why: Closes the local loop and proves the iron rule: a single canonical copy of a skill and an MCP server fans out to all three agents with no hand-copying. This is the foundation every later stage builds on — and the first concrete refutation of the vendor-drift failure mode (Mise 0.7.3 vs vendored 0.7.2).
brief.what: A working repo skeleton (skills/, mcp/, plugins/, servers.yaml, scripts/, Makefile, AGENTS.md+CLAUDE.md, marketplace.json) where 'make sync' regenerates every per-agent view from canonical sources, plus a runnable ping MCP and a couvert skill.
brief.done: Fresh clone + one 'make sync' makes couvert appear as a skill and ping callable in Claude (verified live) and projects correct files for Codex & Antigravity (verified by file inspection; Codex not installed here). ping returns deterministic pong over stdio. No file outside skills/ or mcp/ was hand-edited.
brief.how: Build fresh and public (this repo). One skill (couvert) + one trivial MCP (ping) as canonical sources. 'npx skills' projects skills into .claude/skills/ + .agents/skills/; render-configs.py turns servers.yaml into the 3 MCP configs (Claude .mcp.json, Antigravity mcp_config.json, Codex config.toml). 'make sync' runs both. Flatten canonical skills into plugins/*/skills/ as REAL files (not symlinks) for later org-sync safety.

title: Build the generation pipeline: servers.yaml, render-configs, flatten, bump, Makefile

brief.why: This is the machinery that makes every per-agent view generated rather than hand-copied — the mechanism that enforces the iron rule.
brief.done: 'make sync' runs clean; render-configs produces all three config files with correct shapes; flatten copies real files.
brief.how: servers.yaml: one entry per MCP (ping; transport stdio; command uvx; args). scripts/render-configs.py: servers.yaml -> Claude .mcp.json (mcpServers shape) + Antigravity mcp_config.json + Codex config.toml (TOML via tomli-w / tomllib). scripts/flatten.py: copy skills/<n> -> plugins/<n>/skills/<n> as REAL files. scripts/bump.py + version.py: read/bump plugin.json semver. Makefile: 'sync' (npx skills add . -a claude-code -a codex -a antigravity + render-configs), 'flatten', 'release'. Harden the build-plan sketches; write scripts with uv PEP-723 inline metadata where they need deps.

steps: Write servers.yaml (ping; uv run launcher; transport stdio)
Run make sync and verify the three configs render correctly

title: Verify the local loop: fresh clone + make sync appears in all three agents

brief.why: This is the Stage-0 benchmark — the whole proof. A clean clone must regenerate every view from canonical sources with one command.
brief.what: A reproducible 'make sync' that closes the local loop, plus a verification record.
brief.done: Fresh clone + make sync -> couvert skill + ping MCP present and correct for all three agents (Claude live; Codex/Antigravity by inspection where not runnable); nothing hand-copied.
brief.how: Commit everything; clone fresh to a temp dir; run 'make sync'; confirm .claude/skills/couvert + .agents/skills/couvert exist with real content; confirm .mcp.json / mcp_config.json / config.toml rendered correctly. Verify ping runs over stdio. Verify couvert loads as a skill in Claude (live). Codex not installed -> verify by file inspection and note the gap.
